hadith-eng: Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) said:"Whoever leaves wealth then it is for his heirs, and whoever leaves poor dependents then it (the responsibility) is for me

verification_reason: The text directly addresses the distribution of wealth after a person's death, specifically mentioning the responsibility of the heirs and the Prophet (peace be upon him) towards poor dependents.

hadith-eng: Jabir bin 'Abdullah said:"The wife of Sa'd bin Ar-Rabi came with her two daughters from Sa'd to he Messenger of Allah(S.A.W)and said; O Messenger of Allah(S.A.W)! these two are daughters of Sa'd bin Ar-Rabi who fought along with you on the day of Uhud and was martyred. Their uncle took their wealth, without leaving any wealth for them, and they will not be married unless they have wealth.' He said: 'Allah will decide on that matter.' The ayah about inheritance was revealed, so the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) sent (word) to their Uncle saying: Give the two daughters of Sa'd two thirds, and give their mother one eighth, and whatever remains, then it is for you
question: What is the significance of inheritance laws in Islam as demonstrated in this situation?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of inheritance laws in Islam by providing a specific example where the Prophet (S.A.W) intervened to ensure the daughters of Sa'd bin Ar-Rabi received their rightful inheritance.

hadith-eng: Huzail bin Shurahbil said:"A man came to Abu Musa and Salman bin Rabiah and asked them about a daughter, a son's daughter, a father's sister and a mother's sister. So they said: 'For the daughter is half, for the sister of the father and the mother is what remains.' And they said to him: Go to Abdullah (bin Masud) and ask him, for surely he will concur with us.' So he went to 'Abdullah mentioning that to him and informing him what they had said. 'Abdulah said: 'If that were the case, then I would ave erred and not been among the rightly-guided (on the matter). Rather, I will judge with what the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) judged: For the daughter is half, for the son's daughter a sixth, totaling two-thirds and for the sister is what remains
question: What is the significance of consulting knowledgeable individuals in Islamic jurisprudence?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear example of consulting knowledgeable individuals in Islamic jurisprudence, highlighting the importance of seeking knowledge from those who have been guided by the Messenger of Allah.

hadith-eng: Al-Harith narrated that 'Ali said:"You recite this ayah: After payment of legacies he(or she) may have bequeathed or debts, without causing harm. And indeed the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) judged the debt before the will and that the children (sons and daughters)from the same mother and father inherit,not the sons from various mothers. The man inherits from his brother from his father, and his mother, not his brother from his father. Another chain reports a similar narration
question: What is the significance of prioritizing debts over wills in Islamic inheritance law?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of prioritizing debts over wills in Islamic inheritance law, as per the ayah and the judgment of the Messenger of Allah.

How shall I divide my wealth among my children?' But he did not say anything to me, until the following was revealed: Allah commands you regarding your children's (inheritance): to the male, a portion equal to that of two females

verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a specific example of how divine revelation determines the rules of inheritance in Islam.

hadith-eng: Jabir bin 'Abdullah said:"I was ill, so the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W)came to visit me and found me unconscious. He came walking while Abu Bakr and 'Umar were with him. The Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) performed Wudu, then poured the remaining water on me, so I came to my senses. I said: 'O Messenger of Allah(S.A.W)! how shall I dispose of my wealth?' - or - 'What shall I do with my wealth?' He did not reply anything to me" -and he had nine sisters- "until the Ayah about the inheritance was revealed: they ask you for a legal verdict. Say: "Allah directs (thus) about Al-Kalalah." Jabir said: "It was revealed regarding me
question: What actions did the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) take when visiting a sick person according to this narration?
verification_reason: The narration describes the Prophet's actions when visiting a sick person, including performing Wudu and pouring water on the sick person.

hadith-eng: Imran bin Husain narrated:"A man came to the Prophet (S.A.W) and said" 'My son died, so what do I inherit from him?' He said: 'For you is a sixth.' When he turned to leave,he called him and said: 'For you is another sixth.' So when he turned to leave , he called him saying: 'The last sixth is consumable for you
question: What is the Islamic ruling on inheritance when a child passes away?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing the Islamic ruling on inheritance when a child passes away.

hadith-eng: Qabisah bin Dhuw'aib said:"A grandmother - the mother of a mother, or the mother of a father - came to Abu Bakr and she said: 'a son of my son' - or, 'a son of my daughter died, and I have been informed that there is a right ( from the wealth) for me in the Book.' So Abu Bakr said: 'I do not find that there is a right for you in the Book, and I have not heard that the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) judged anything for you. I shall ask the people.' So, Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah testified that the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) gave her (case) a sixth. He said: 'And who heard that along with you?' He said: 'Muhammad bin Maslamah.'" He said: "So he gave her a sixth. Then the other grandmother who was left behind came to 'Umar." Sufyan said: "And Ma'mar said to me in addition, from Az-Zuhri - and I do not remember it to be from Az-Zuhri, rather I remember it to be from Ma'mar - that 'Umar said: 'If the two of you are together then it is for both of you, and whichever of you is alone with it (the sixth), then it is for her
question: What is the significance of consulting others when making a decision in Islamic jurisprudence?
verification_reason: The text illustrates the importance of consulting others, specifically the Companions of the Prophet, when making a decision in Islamic jurisprudence.

hadith-eng: Qabisah bin Dhuw'aib said:"A grandmother came to Abu Bakr to ask him about her inheritance. He said to her, 'There is noting for you in the Book of Allah and there is nothing for you in the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W). So ,return until I ask the people. So he asked the people and Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah said: 'I was present when the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) gave her (case) a sixth.' So he said: 'Was anyone else with you?' Muhammad bin Maslamah stood to say the same as what Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah said. So Abu Bakr implemented that for her." Then the other grandmother came to 'Umar bin Al-Khattab to ask him about her inheritance. He said: 'There is nothing in the Book of Allah for you, but there is that sixth. So if the two of you are together then it is for both of you, and whichever of you remains), then it is for her

verification_reason: The answer explains the process of determining inheritance shares when there is no explicit mention in the Quran or Sunnah, by referring to the practice of the Prophet (S.A.W) and the subsequent implementation by Abu Bakr and 'Umar bin Al-Khattab.

Their uncle took their wealth, without leaving any wealth for them, and they will not be married unless they have wealth.' He said: 'Allah will decide on that matter.' hadith-eng: Huzail bin Shurahbil said:"A man came to Abu Musa and Salman bin Rabiah and asked them about a daughter, a son's daughter, a father's sister and a mother's sister. So they said: 'For the daughter is half, for the sister of the father and the mother is what remains.' And they said to him: Go to Abdullah (bin Masud) and ask him, for surely he will concur with us.' So he went to 'Abdullah mentioning that to him and informing him what they had said. 'Abdulah said: 'If that were the case, then I would ave erred and not been among the rightly-guided (on the matter). Rather, I will judge with what the Messenger of Allah(S.A.W) judged: For the daughter is half, for the son's daughter a sixth, totaling two-thirds and for the sister is what remains
